3+ Bedroom Units  1 units 3+ Bedroom Units | 1 units | 101.5.1 All Dwelling Units (1) The minimum number of dwelling units with at least 3 bedrooms must be provided on a lot as follows: Total Dwelling Units on a Lot 1 to 3 Units Minimum 3+ Bedroom Units: 1 Unit

3+ Bedroom Units  2 units 3+ Bedroom Units | 2 units | 101.5.1 All Dwelling Units (1) The minimum number of dwelling units with at least 3 bedrooms must be provided on a lot as follows: Total Dwelling Units on a Lot 4 to 6 Units Minimum 3+ Bedroom Units: 2 Units
lot  6 units This District provides for the use and development of primarily ground-oriented residential dwellings up to a maximum of 6 dwelling units on a lot.

lot  This District provides for the use and development of primarily ground-oriented residential dwellings up to a maximum of 6 dwelling units on a lot. This District provides for the use and development of primarily ground-oriented residential dwellings up to a maximum of 6 dwelling units on a lot.

subdivision plan  .1 At the time of registration of the subdivision plan to create two or more rowhouse lots, the registration of a Section 219 Covenant will be required to ensure that all adjoining rowhouse dwellings will be constructed at the same time. .1 At the time of registration of the subdivision plan to create two or more rowhouse lots, the registration of a Section 219 Covenant will be required to ensure that all adjoining rowhouse dwellings will be constructed at the same time.

lot  .2 Panhandle lots or other irregular lot configurations are not permitted except in accordance with Section 101.6.4 or where constrained by restrictive site conditions as determined by the Approving Officer. .2 Panhandle lots or other irregular lot configurations are not permitted except in accordance with Section 101.6.4 or where constrained by restrictive site conditions as determined by the Approving Officer.
lot area  280 m² .3 Rowhouse lots may exceed 280 m² subject to Section 101.4 footnote .1.

Maximum Lot Area lot area  280 m² .1 Maximum lot area for rowhouse dwellings may exceed 280 m², subject to registration of a Section 219 Covenant prohibiting the construction of more than 3 dwelling units on the lot.
permitted projections  Sections 101.6.1 and 6.12 .2 See Sections 101.6.1 and 6.12 for permitted projections into required yards
additional setbacks  Section 6.13 see Section 6.13 for additional setbacks that may be required for vision clearance areas.

interior side yard setback  .3 Interior side yard setbacks are not required for rowhouse dwelling units on a lot line between two adjoining rowhouse dwellings with a shared party wall. .3 Interior side yard setbacks are not required for rowhouse dwelling units on a lot line between two adjoining rowhouse dwellings with a shared party wall.
interior side yard setback  1.2 m For an end unit of a rowhouse development, the interior side yard setback is 1.2 m.
location  within the minimum separations between principal buildings .4 Accessory buildings are not permitted within the minimum separations between principal buildings
permitted projections  Section 101.6.1 see Section 101.6.1 for permitted projections into minimum separations.
separation from front and rear principals  6.0 m Any principal building located between a front and rear principal must have a minimum 6.0 m separation from each of the front and rear principals
separation from opposing front principals  6.0 m or, in the case of through lots, from each opposing front principal
separation from other principals between it and a side lot line  2.4 m and a minimum 2.4 m separation from any other principals between it and a side lot line.

main entrance orientation  1 dwelling unit (2) At least one dwelling unit on a lot must have the main entrance oriented to an abutting street or, in the case of a through lot with multiple principal buildings and site access from both frontages, at least one dwelling unit per street frontage.

main entrance orientation  1 dwelling unit per street frontage (2) At least one dwelling unit on a lot must have the main entrance oriented to an abutting street or, in the case of a through lot with multiple principal buildings and site access from both frontages, at least one dwelling unit per street frontage.

illuminated and publicly accessible pedestrian walkway spacing  100 m (1) Contiguous rowhouse lots must provide an illuminated and publicly accessible pedestrian walkway spaced every 100 m or less from a street, lane, or walkway that intersects the fronting street and that:

pedestrian walkway linkage  links the fronting street with the parallel lane, street, or trail network at the rear of the lot (a) links the fronting street with the parallel lane, street, or trail network at the rear of the lot;
pedestrian walkway width  1.5 m (b) is at least 1.5 m wide and clear to a height of at least 2.5 m; and
pedestrian walkway clear height  2.5 m (b) is at least 1.5 m wide and clear to a height of at least 2.5 m; and
pedestrian walkway right of way  subject to a statutory right of way registered in favour of the City (c) is subject to a statutory right of way registered in favour of the City.

Boarding, lodging or rooming houses  permitted (1) Boarding, lodging or rooming houses are permitted for not more than 5 persons, when situated in a single family dwelling on a lot with an area of not less than 560 m², subject to the condition that it is included as part of a comprehensive development plan to which the provisions of the Comprehensive Development District apply.

capacity  5 persons (1) Boarding, lodging or rooming houses are permitted for not more than 5 persons, when situated in a single family dwelling on a lot with an area of not less than 560 m², subject to the condition that it is included as part of a comprehensive development plan to which the provisions of the Comprehensive Development District apply.

lot area  560 m² (1) Boarding, lodging or rooming houses are permitted for not more than 5 persons, when situated in a single family dwelling on a lot with an area of not less than 560 m², subject to the condition that it is included as part of a comprehensive development plan to which the provisions of the Comprehensive Development District apply.
Principal Use Category A Supportive Housing facilities  permitted (1) Category A Supportive Housing facilities are permitted on a lot as the only principal use, subject to the following conditions:
living unit floor area  27 m² (a) each living unit shall have a minimum floor area of 27 m²;

Category A Supportive Housing facilities  meets the development regulations under Section 101.4 for small-scale multi-unit lots with 1 to 3 units (b) it meets the development regulations under Section 101.4 for small-scale multi-unit lots with 1 to 3 units; and

Category A Supportive Housing facilities  included as part of a comprehensive development plan to which the provisions of the Comprehensive Development District apply (c) the use shall be included as part of a comprehensive development plan to which the provisions of the Comprehensive Development District apply.

Parking location  in an attached or detached garage, carport or outdoor parking pad at the rear of the lot (a) Parking shall be provided in an attached or detached garage, carport or outdoor parking pad at the rear of the lot when accessible from an abutting lane or street. Where there is no direct access to the rear of the lot or the Director Engineering is satisfied that access to the rear is not feasible because of an extreme grade, parking may be provided elsewhere on the lot.

garage or carport  constructed below grade (b) A garage or carport may be constructed below grade, provided that no part of such structure shall extend more than 1.2 m above the surface of the surrounding ground at any point other than the driveway, nor be less than 1.2 m from a lot line.

extension above surface of surrounding ground  1.2 m (b) A garage or carport may be constructed below grade, provided that no part of such structure shall extend more than 1.2 m above the surface of the surrounding ground at any point other than the driveway, nor be less than 1.2 m from a lot line.

setback from lot line  1.2 m (b) A garage or carport may be constructed below grade, provided that no part of such structure shall extend more than 1.2 m above the surface of the surrounding ground at any point other than the driveway, nor be less than 1.2 m from a lot line.

accessory structure not being a building  4.6 m An accessory structure not being a building, excluding a fence or a retaining wall, located outside of a required yard, shall not exceed 4.6 m in height.
child care facility  25 children (a) be limited to a maximum of 25 children;
child care facility  located on a corner lot (b) be located on a corner lot;

child care facility  comply with the development regulations under section 101.4 for 1 to 3 small-scale multi-unit dwelling units on a lot (c) comply with the development regulations under section 101.4 for 1 to 3 small-scale multi-unit dwelling units on a lot;

Principal Use child care facility  located on a lot that does not contain a dwelling unit or any other principal use (d) be located on a lot that does not contain a dwelling unit or any other principal use; and
child care facility  comply with all other applicable regulations under this Bylaw (e) comply with all other applicable regulations under this Bylaw.
steps and stairs  permitted (a) steps and stairs;

ornamental features, such as arbors, trellises, fish ponds, flag poles, or similar landscape features  permitted (b) ornamental features, such as arbors, trellises, fish ponds, flag poles, or similar landscape features;
terraces, decks, or other similar surfaces  permitted (c) terraces, decks, or other similar surfaces that are 1.0 m or less above grade;

balconies, covered decks, uncovered decks, canopies, sunshades, or other similar features, including supporting structures  25 % of the width of a required separation (d) balconies, covered decks, uncovered decks, canopies, sunshades, or other similar features, including supporting structures, that are greater than 1.0 m above grade up to a maximum of 25% of the width of a required separation;

belt courses, cornices, gutters, sills, chimneys, bay windows, or other similar features  0.9 m (e) belt courses, cornices, gutters, sills, chimneys, bay windows, or other similar features up to the lesser of 0.9 m or 25% of the width of a required separation;

belt courses, cornices, gutters, sills, chimneys, bay windows, or other similar features  25 % of the width of a required separation (e) belt courses, cornices, gutters, sills, chimneys, bay windows, or other similar features up to the lesser of 0.9 m or 25% of the width of a required separation;
sunken access areas and window wells  as per Section 6.9 (f) sunken access areas and window wells as per Section 6.9;
outdoor appliances  permitted (g) outdoor appliances; and
eaves  1.2 m (h) eaves up to the lesser of 1.2 m (3.94 ft.) or 25% of the width of a required separation.
eaves  25 % of the width of a required separation (h) eaves up to the lesser of 1.2 m (3.94 ft.) or 25% of the width of a required separation.

Permitted projections into required yards  subject to Section 6.12 Permitted projections into required yards are subject to Section 6.12, except that in the R1 District projections into required lane yards are limited to a maximum of 0.8 m to provide adequate fire truck clearance.

projections into required lane yards  0.8 m Permitted projections into required yards are subject to Section 6.12, except that in the R1 District projections into required lane yards are limited to a maximum of 0.8 m to provide adequate fire truck clearance.
projections into required lane yards  0.8 m except that in the R1 District projections into required lane yards are limited to a maximum of 0.8 m to provide adequate fire truck clearance.

outdoor amenity space  2.0 m An outdoor amenity space with a minimum width of 2.0 m and area of 10.0 m² must be provided for each primary dwelling unit for its exclusive use and be accessible from the primary dwelling unit it serves.

outdoor amenity space  10.0 m² An outdoor amenity space with a minimum width of 2.0 m and area of 10.0 m² must be provided for each primary dwelling unit for its exclusive use and be accessible from the primary dwelling unit it serves.

automatic sprinkler system  (1) Dwelling units located more than 45 m from a lot line abutting a street shall contain an automatic sprinkler system. (1) Dwelling units located more than 45 m from a lot line abutting a street shall contain an automatic sprinkler system.
fire access corridor width  1.0 m (2) All dwelling units shall have a minimum 1.0 m paved or gravel fire access corridor that:
fire access corridor material  paved or gravel (2) All dwelling units shall have a minimum 1.0 m paved or gravel fire access corridor that:

direct pedestrian access  direct pedestrian access from the dwelling unit entrance to a lot line abutting a street, or abutting a constructed lane where direct access to a street is not feasible (a) provides direct pedestrian access from the dwelling unit entrance to a lot line abutting a street, or abutting a constructed lane where direct access to a street is not feasible; and
clear height  2.5 m (b) is clear of any projections or obstructions to a minimum of 2.5 m in height.
panhandle lots and other irregularly shaped lots   (a) panhandle lots and other irregularly shaped lots may be permitted subject to the following:
panhandle width  1 m (i) lots with lane access shall have a minimum panhandle width of 1 m that is clear to a height of 2.5 m; and
clear height  2.5 m (i) lots with lane access shall have a minimum panhandle width of 1 m that is clear to a height of 2.5 m; and
panhandle width  4.5 m (ii) lots without lane access shall have a minimum panhandle width of 4.5 m that is clear to a height of 2.5 m;
clear height  2.5 m (ii) lots without lane access shall have a minimum panhandle width of 4.5 m that is clear to a height of 2.5 m;
Maximum Lot Coverage lot coverage  60 % (b) maximum lot coverage as set out in Section 101.4 may be increased up to 60% and impervious surface area up to 70%;
Maximum Lot Coverage impervious surface area  70 % (b) maximum lot coverage as set out in Section 101.4 may be increased up to 60% and impervious surface area up to 70%;

original architectural appurtenances   (c) all original architectural appurtenances, such as chimneys, railings, vents, decorative features, or similar, may be excluded from the maximum permitted height of a principal building;
lot line setbacks  2.0 m (d) lot line setbacks for street yards may meet a minimum of 2.0 m;
minimum separation between buildings  reduced (e) the minimum separation between buildings on the same lot as required under Section 101.4 may be reduced;
Section 101.5.1  (f) Section 101.5.1 does not apply; and (f) Section 101.5.1 does not apply; and
off-street vehicular parking spaces   (i) off-street vehicular parking spaces are not required;
retention and continued use of non-conforming driveways   (ii) retention and continued use of driveways that do not otherwise conform with the requirements of this Bylaw may be permitted; and

parking within required yards   (iii) parking may be provided within the required yards, and any existing parking areas on the lot that do not conform with the requirements of this Bylaw may continue to be used.

continued use of non-conforming parking areas   (iii) parking may be provided within the required yards, and any existing parking areas on the lot that do not conform with the requirements of this Bylaw may continue to be used.

height  lower of the average front or average rear elevation, as defined by the lower of the average natural grade or finished grade along the exterior of the building facing the respective front or rear lot line The height of a principal or accessory building shall be measured from the lower of the average front or average rear elevation, as defined by the lower of the average natural grade or finished grade along the exterior of the building facing the respective front or rear lot line
height  midpoint of a sloped roof or the highest point of a flat the roof to the midpoint of a sloped roof or the highest point of a flat the roof.

midpoint  one half of the vertical distance of a sloped roof structure measured from the top plate of the uppermost storey to the highest point of the roof The midpoint of a sloped roof is considered to be one half of the vertical distance of a sloped roof structure measured from the top plate of the uppermost storey to the highest point of the roof.
height  finished grade at the point used for vehicular access The height of a detached garage or carport shall be measured from the finished grade at the point used for vehicular access.
separation distances  nearest point of each building face Required separation distances between buildings are measured from the nearest point of each building face.

Off-Street Parking  Schedule 8 (b) Schedule 8: Off-Street Parking
